sample.title: How Data Centers in Space Could Change AI
sample.description:
“Data centers in space” may sound like science fiction, but as the SpaceX IPO augurs a space boom, tech giants and startups are betting orbital artificial intelligence is the next frontier.

How to Build a Data Center in Space: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn
Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos plan to make AI extraterrestrial.

Building data centers in space is still a relatively nascent idea, but it’s gaining traction and financial backing, as energy and space constraints limit the expansion of massive computing facilities here on Earth. Earlier this year, in the latest leg of their decades-long space race, Elon Musk’s SpaceX and Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin both announced plans to build and launch so-called orbital data centers.

Learn more: https://bloom.bg/4fSeMXn

01:37-03:43 Starcloud’s big ambition

sample.title: Dario Amodei Addresses Reports of Claude’s Role in US Military Strikes
sample.description:
On The Circuit with Emily Chang, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei addresses reports that the US military is using Claude as part of the US-Israel war with Iran, including during a strike in Iran that destroyed a girls' school, killing more than 150 people. 

Amodei concedes "terrible" mistakes will be made but defends the integration of AI in defense by emphasizing that "a human makes the final call."

sample.title: Inside Indonesia's Market Meltdown
sample.description:
Indonesia was once seen as a darling of investors. At $1.5 trillion, Indonesia’s GDP is more than that of Singapore and Thailand combined. Until recently, Indonesia’s stock exchange was the biggest in the region. 

But fears of corruption and policy missteps in markets and government under President Prabowo Subianto are raising concern about the country’s economic future.

sample.title: Why Abercrombie Is Copying Aritzia's Playbook
sample.description:
Abercrombie & Fitch's torrid growth has started to slow. To counteract moderating sales and increasing competition, the retailer is selling third-party shoe brands like Puma, Frye, Hunter and GH Bass. CNBC's Gabrielle Fonrouge reports.

sample.title: Where Markets Think Fed Chair Warsh Is Taking Interest Rates
sample.description:

sample.title: Can AI Compute Become The Next Big Futures Market?
sample.description:
For decades, companies have turned to futures markets to manage uncertainty. Airlines hedge fuel costs. Farmers hedge crops. Manufacturers hedge metals. Now a startup wants to bring that same financial machinery to artificial intelligence. Silicon Data and CME Group are working on futures contracts tied to GPU rental prices, a market that could help firms hedge rising AI infrastructure, cloud computing and data center costs. AI companies spend heavily to rent high-end GPU power for training and running artificial intelligence models, but future compute prices can be hard to predict.  CNBC's Yun Li reports.

sample.title: Why KFC Has Fallen Behind In The U.S.
sample.description:
Chains like Chick-fil-A and Raising Cane's have become fast food mainstays as Americans continue to crave chicken. Yet KFC has become the fourth-largest U.S. chicken chain by market share despite the company saying that ‘it made chicken famous.’ The U.S. has also slipped to its third-largest market behind China and Europe. Now it's rebranding its logos, menu and restaurants as it hopes to reinvigorate sales and cut through the crowded fast-food sector.  CNBC’s Amelia Lucas has more on the company’s innovations.

Produced Shot and Edited by: Natalie Rice
Reporting by: Amelia Lucas
Senior Managing Producer: Tala Hadavi
Additional Footage: Getty Images, KFC

sample.title: What To Expect From Trump’s Trip To The G7 Summit In France
sample.description:
President Trump is set to travel to France for the upcoming G7 summit, which will take place from June 15-17. Markets will be watching for signs of stability as the summit takes place against the backdrop of the Iran war, the Russia-Ukraine war and tensions between the U.S. and Europe over tariffs. CNBC’s Megan Cassella breaks down what to expect from the summit.

Reporter: Megan Cassella
Produced by: Macklin Fishman, Ashlee Trujillo
Edited by: Andrea Miller

sample.title: Ebola mourners learn how to grieve safely after outbreak in DR Congo | BBC News
sample.description:
Mourners in DR Congo are learning how to grieve safely, as large traditional funerals are scrapped in a bid to prevent the spread of the Ebola virus.

The outbreak has so far killed almost 200 people in the last few months, mainly in the province of Ituri of which Bunia is the capital.
 
Ebola is transmitted through contact with infected bodily fluids and stringent protocols have to be followed to stop it spreading - including safe burials.
 
One traditional practice that is now strongly discouraged is the washing of dead bodies by family members before burial.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

For the latest news download the BBC News app or visit BBC.com/news

#BBCNews

Young women now have 'close to zero' risk of cervical cancer death after HPV jab | BBC News
sample.description:
Children vaccinated at age 12–13 against HPV (human papillomavirus) have close to zero risk of dying from cervical cancer before the age of 30, landmark new research reveals.
 
The first study of its kind shows deaths have fallen sharply since school-age girls began being offered it in 2008, and around 200 lives have been saved in England so far thanks to the vaccine.
 
Between 2020 and 2024, no cervical cancer deaths were recorded in women aged 20 to 24 - the first time that had happened over a five-year period.

Subscribe to our channel here: https://bbc.in/bbcnews

sample.title: Cervical cancer deaths in England reach historic low. #BBCNews
